name: @forwardimpact/pathway
description: Explore roles, generate agent profiles, and surface career expectations so progression and team configuration are visible, not guessed.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: Two managers disagree on what 'senior' means — neither can point to a written definition.
jobs.bigHire: define what good engineering means so roles have clear, defensible expectations.
jobs.littleHire: define or update a role's expectations knowing structural mistakes get caught.
jobs.competesWith: tribal knowledge; borrowed frameworks; per-manager intuition; tolerating the ambiguity

forces.pull: A shared definition that makes quality visible and discussable.
forces.habit: Each manager carries a private mental model of what 'good' means.
forces.anxiety: Getting definitions wrong feels worse than having none.

jobs.firedWhen: definitions drift from practice; or a reorg removes the mandate.

jobs.trigger: A post-mortem surfaces the same skill gap that caused the last incident — nobody saw it before staffing.
jobs.bigHire: make staffing decisions I can defend by seeing what each role requires.
jobs.littleHire: spot capability gaps and check whether a candidate fills them.
jobs.competesWith: résumé screening; gut feel; copying a team that seems to work; accepting gaps as inevitable

forces.push: Capability gaps appear as incidents, never in advance.
forces.pull: Confidence that a staffing change strengthens the team as a system.
forces.habit: Hiring on résumés and gut rather than team composition analysis.

jobs.firedWhen: the skill taxonomy drifts from actual roles; or a hiring freeze removes staffing pressure.

jobs.trigger: 'Meets expectations' on the review form has no definition anyone can point to.
jobs.bigHire: see exactly what's expected at my level so I stop guessing during reviews.
jobs.littleHire: understand the autonomy and scope my level implies.
jobs.competesWith: asking peers; inferring from manager comments; browsing LinkedIn; living with ambiguity

forces.push: Ambiguous expectations create anxiety about reviews and readiness.
forces.pull: Clarity about what's expected, removing the guesswork from self-assessment.
forces.habit: Inferring expectations from peer behavior rather than a standard.
forces.anxiety: Looking up the standard might confirm gaps easier left ignored.

jobs.firedWhen: defined expectations diverge from actual promotions; or a reorganization makes the framework feel irrelevant.

jobs.trigger: An agent's work was rejected because it followed generic practices instead of the organization's standards.
jobs.bigHire: configure agents to meet the expectations the organization holds for humans.
jobs.littleHire: give agents organizational context without bespoke prompts.
jobs.competesWith: custom system prompts; copying another team's config; hoping generic instructions work

forces.push: Generic configuration misses what the organization expects.
forces.pull: Agents that reflect organizational standards without bespoke prompts.
forces.habit: Treating agent configuration as a one-time setup, not something that evolves.
forces.anxiety: Over-constraining agents might limit their usefulness on novel tasks.

jobs.firedWhen: configuration overhead exceeds the quality gain; or policy bans custom configuration.

jobs.trigger: Reviewing agent output line by line because there's no way to tell whether it meets the quality bar.
jobs.bigHire: verify agent work against the standard so I review by exception, not by default.
jobs.littleHire: check expected output and get a second opinion before approving a deliverable.
jobs.competesWith: reviewing every line; accepting slower delivery; trusting uncritically; not using agents for substantive work

forces.push: Full review of every deliverable negates the productivity gain.
forces.pull: Confident delegation — knowing output meets the standard without reading every line.
forces.habit: Reviewing everything because that's how quality has always been caught.
forces.anxiety: Delegating quality judgment might miss context-specific issues.

jobs.firedWhen: verification catches fewer issues than full review; or the team stops using agents.
